channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Berlin police say they have detained a man who attacked 2 people with a knife
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Berlin police said they detained a man Tuesday who attacked and injured two people in the city's Charlottenburg neighborhood, further jolting Germany days after a deadly Christmas market attack elsewhere in the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Montenegro extradites cryptocurrency mogul Do Kwon to the United States
item.description: PODGORICA, Montenegro (AP) — Montenegro on Tuesday extradited a South Korean mogul known as “the cryptocurrency king” to the United States, following a decision of its justice ministry earlier this month to accept a U.S.
item.dc:creator: PREDRAG MILIC, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Have any New Year's resolutions? Here are some tips to help make them stick
item.description: Studies have shown that up to 70% of people who make New Year’s resolutions abandon those good intentions within months. Here are some tips for how to keep those resolutions when your willpower starts to falter.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: The Pentagon chief loses bid to reject 9/11 plea deals
item.description: A military appeals court has ruled against Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin's effort to throw out the plea deals reached for Khalid Sheikh Mohammed and two other defendants in the 9/11 attacks.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Authorities issue NYE warning as nation’s top tourist spot becomes a high-threat zone and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 6 suspects allegedly involved in deadly human smuggling incidents over the past week, Coast Guard says
item.description: United States Coast Guard officials said six people are suspected in a fatal human smuggling event involving more than 60 people last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: LIVE: Cities across the globe welcome the New Year in celebrations tinged with local traditions
item.description: Cities around the world are readying to ring in the New Year with celebrations highlighting local cultures and traditions, after a year roiled by ongoing conflict and political instability.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter made eradicating Guinea worm disease a top mission
item.description: JARWENG, South Sudan (AP) — Nobel Prize-winning peacemaker Jimmy Carter spent nearly four decades waging war to eliminate an ancient parasite plaguing the world’s poorest people.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How Jimmy Carter paved way for craft beer
item.description: A bill signed by then-President Jimmy Carter helped spark America's craft beer industry. Panelists weigh in on his legacy.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: NYPD hunting for suspect after baby abandoned in bag on NYC street
item.description: Police are searching for a person they say left an infant girl in a tote bag Sunday morning in the Bronx.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge denies sentencing delay for ex-Sen. Bob Menendez but puts his wife's trial off until February
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former New Jersey Sen. Bob Menendez will be sentenced on corruption charges as scheduled at the end of January, and his wife’s trial will be moved from January to February, a judge said Monday. U.S. District Judge Sidney H.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Convicted South Carolina bank killer asks for 'compassionate release' days after Biden commuted death sentence
item.description: A convicted killer who ruthlessly gunned down two South Carolina bank employees in 2017 has demanded to be released from prison days after President Biden saved him from death row.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Appeals court upholds verdict finding Trump liable for sexually assaulting E. Jean Carroll
item.description: A federal appeals court panel on Monday upheld a jury’s verdict finding President-elect Donald Trump liable for sexually assaulting advice columnist E. Jean Carroll and ordering him to pay $5 million.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Cats can get sick with bird flu. Here's how to protect them
item.description: The death of an Oregon house cat and a pet food recall are raising questions about the ongoing outbreak of bird flu and how people can protect their pets.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Southerners are cleaning up from tornadoes that swept the region
item.description: Cleanup is underway after a strong storm system spawned hail, rain, high winds and tornadoes across the southern U.S. over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Stengle And Lekan Oyekanmi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Northern lights could be visible in upper fringes of the US this New Year's Eve
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — There's a chance solar storms may bring northern lights to several northern U.S states just in time for the new year. The sun expelled two bursts of plasma that are hurtling toward Earth and are expected to arrive early this week, according to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The world population will be 8.09 billion on New Year's Day after a 71 million increase in 2024
item.description: The world population increased by more than 71 million people in 2024 and will be 8.09 billion people on New Year's Day, according to U.S. Census Bureau estimates released Monday. The 0.9% increase in 2024 was a slight slowdown from 2023, when the world population grew by 75 million people.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Census Bureau releases 'population clock' projection for New Year's Day
item.description: Based on current estimates, there will also be one birth every 9 seconds and one death every 9.4 seconds in January 2025.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Northern lights could be visible in upper fringes of the US on New Year's Eve
item.description: There's a chance solar storms may bring northern lights to several northern U.S states just in time for the new year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California ‘micronation’ offering reward after fathers, children allegedly burglarize, vandalize property
item.description: The “micronation” of Slowjamastan, located in Southern California, is offering a $1,000 cash reward to anyone who can help bring to justice a group of fathers and their children who allegedly burglarized and vandalized their property.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Washington inmate accused of molesting cellmate after changing gender, transfer to women's prison
item.description: A former Washington inmate is suing the state and prison officials after she says they forced her into a cell with a biological male who molested her.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea struggles to find cause of plane crash that killed 179
item.description: South Korean officials said Monday they will conduct safety inspections of all Boeing 737-800 aircraft operated by the country's airlines as they struggle to determine what caused a plane crash that killed 179 people a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter, the 39th US president, has died at 100
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Jimmy Carter, the peanut farmer who won the presidency in the wake of the Watergate scandal and Vietnam War, endured humbling defeat after one tumultuous term and then redefined life after the White House as a global humanitarian, has died. He was 100 years old.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and ALEX SANZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Anti-Israel agitators terrorize Americans: See 2024's most extreme moments
item.description: A list of some of the craziest moments during anti-Israel protests in 2024 amid the war between the Jewish state and the Hamas terror group.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bills clinch the AFC's No. 2 seed with a 40-14 rout of the undisciplined Jets
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — Josh Allen reflected on Buffalo’s many offseason critics and doubters when asked about the Bills achieving their latest milestones while sewing up the AFC’s No. 2 seed with a 40-14 rout of the unraveling and undisciplined New York Jets on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jetliner skids off runway and bursts into flames while landing in South Korea, killing 179
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A jetliner skidded off a runway, slammed into a concrete fence and burst into flames Sunday in South Korea after its landing gear apparently failed to deploy. All but two of the 181 people aboard were killed in one of the country’s worst aviation disasters, officials said.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The Latest: State funeral for Jimmy Carter will be Jan. 9
item.description: Former President Jimmy Carter has died at the age of 100. The 39th president of the United States was a Georgia peanut farmer who sought to restore trust in government when he assumed the presidency in 1977 and then built a reputation for tireless work as a humanitarian.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Photos: Jimmy Carter, from the White House to building houses
item.description: From The White House to building houses, here's a look at former president Jimmy Carter's life in photos.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nasty norovirus is back in full force with US cases of the stomach virus surging
item.description: Cases of a wretched stomach bug are surging in parts of the United States this winter, according to government data.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Former NCAA quarterback Tommy Lazzaro dies in 'tragic' accident at US Air Force base
item.description: Tommy Lazzaro, an Army sergeant and former quarterback for the Central Michigan Chippewas, has died following an accident at Eglin Air Force Base in Florida. He was 27.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, ABC 4

item.title: Stunning photos show lava erupting from Hawaii's Kilauea volcano
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — One of the world's most active volcanoes spewed lava into the air for a second straight day on Tuesday. The eruption of Kilauea volcano on Hawaii's Big Island has stayed within the mountain's summit caldera inside Hawaii Volcanoes National Park. No homes were threatened.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
